description_long: This object is a large, militarized, tripod-mounted automatic rifle system with a robust, olive-drab green exterior. It features a heavy, multi-barreled rotary gun mounted on a central turret, supported by three articulated mechanical legs with wide, stable feet for deployment on urban surfaces. The central body houses a cylindrical mechanism, likely for ammunition feed and targeting electronics, with visible cabling and reinforced armor plating. The front of the turret includes a prominent sensor or camera module, possibly for autonomous targeting or remote operation. The legs are jointed and reinforced, suggesting the ability to adjust height and stance for stability on uneven ground. The overall design is utilitarian and rugged, optimized for durability and sustained fire in urban combat scenarios.
description: A large, tripod-mounted, multi-barreled automatic rifle system with articulated legs and a robust olive-green armored body, designed for urban combat.
description_view_0: Front view shows the central turret with a prominent sensor module and the symmetrical arrangement of the three mechanical legs.
description_view_1: Side view highlights the multi-barreled rotary gun extending forward and the cylindrical mechanism at the turret's core.
description_view_2: Rear view reveals the back of the turret, structural supports, and the rear articulation of the legs.
description_view_3: Opposite side view displays the rotary gun from the other side and the detailed leg mechanisms.
